ddls:
CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the application */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the application */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the application started */,
  "app_type" TEXT /* Type of application (e.g., desktop, mobile, web) */,
  "dev_team" TEXT /* Development team responsible for the application */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment of the application (e.g., production, staging, testing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "processes" (
  "process_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each process */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application the process belongs to */,
  "upid" INTEGER /* User Process ID associated with the process */,
  "ts" TEXT /* Timestamp of the recorded metrics */,
  "path" TEXT /* Path of the executable file */,
  "size_kb" INTEGER /* Size of memory allocated to the process in KB */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the process (e.g., running, sleeping, zombie) */,
  "threads" INTEGER /* Number of threads in the process */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the process */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("process_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_processes_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory usage record */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the process being monitored */,
  "private_dirty_kb" INTEGER /* Amount of private dirty memory used in KB */,
  "swap_kb" INTEGER /* Amount of swap memory used in KB */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file associated with the process */,
  "total_memory_kb" INTEGER /* Total memory used by the process in KB */,
  "memory_swapped_kb" INTEGER /* Amount of memory swapped out by the process in KB */,
  "memory_usage_percentage" REAL /* Memory usage percentage of the process */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_usage_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("process_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "file_paths" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file record */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the process associated with the file */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path of the file associated with the process */,
  "protection_flags" INTEGER /* Protection flags for the file */,
  "file_size_kb" INTEGER /* Size of the file in KB */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., executable, text, image) */,
  "file_permissions" TEXT /* Permissions of the file (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_file_paths_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("process_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_residents" (
  "resident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resident memory record */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the process being monitored */,
  "private_clean_resident_kb" INTEGER /* Amount of clean private resident memory in KB */,
  "shared_dirty_resident_kb" INTEGER /* Amount of dirty shared resident memory in KB */,
  "shared_clean_resident_kb" INTEGER /* Amount of clean shared resident memory in KB */,
  "resident_memory_usage_percentage" REAL /* Resident memory usage percentage of the process */,
  "resident_memory_shared_kb" INTEGER /* Amount of resident memory shared with other processes in KB */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_residents_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("process_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the process that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high memory usage) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message detailing the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("process_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "job_function" TEXT /* Job function of the user */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the action taken */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken by the user */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method of access (e.g., web, API, CLI) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric record */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application being monitored */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metrics were recorded */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage percentage */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage percentage */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Disk usage percentage */,
  "network_usage" REAL /* Network usage percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert history record */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert triggered */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on the resolution of the alert */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_history_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_history_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id")
)
